crisis_plan.label: Crisis plan
crisis_plan.title: My Crisis Plan
crisis_plan.subtitle: Fill in this plan with your practitioner. It will help you in difficult moments.
crisis_plan.step_1_hint: How do I feel when a crisis is approaching?
crisis_plan.step_2_hint: What can I do alone to calm myself?
crisis_plan.step_3_hint: Who can I see or where can I go to distract myself?
crisis_plan.step_4_hint: Who can listen and help if I feel in danger?
crisis_plan.step_5_hint: Which professionals or services can I contact?
crisis_plan.step_6_hint: How can I make my surroundings safer?
crisis_plan.anchors_subtitle: Images and a phrase that remind you why life is worth living.
crisis_plan.anchor_phrase_placeholder: A phrase that grounds you in the present…
crisis_plan.coping_cards_subtitle: Personalised strategies prepared with your practitioner.
crisis_plan.coping_cards_empty: Your practitioner has not added any cards yet.
crisis_plan.commitment_phrase_default: I commit to using this plan before any act of self-harm, and to calling my practitioner if I don't feel capable.
crisis_plan.urgency_subtitle: Here are your emergency contacts and resources.
crisis_plan.urgency_no_items: No contacts filled in your plan.
crisis_plan.photo_error: Unable to add photo. Check app permissions in Settings.

therapeutic_commitment.label: Therapeutic commitment
therapeutic_commitment.description: My commitment to using emergency resources

distress_tolerance.label: Distress tolerance
distress_tolerance.description: Emergency techniques for getting through a crisis

medication_side_effects.label: Treatment side effects tracker
medication_side_effects.description: Log your daily side effects (drowsiness, restlessness…)
medication_side_effects.title: Treatment side effects tracker
medication_side_effects.intro: Log any side effects you experience.
medication_side_effects.effect_akathisia_detail: Inability to stay still, inner restlessness
medication_side_effects.effect_tremors_detail: Hands, limbs — may occur with some psychiatric medications
medication_side_effects.effect_dry_mouth_detail: Dry mouth — may occur with some medications
medication_side_effects.effect_sleep_detail: Insomnia or difficulty sleeping — may occur with some antidepressants or mood stabilisers
medication_side_effects.effect_nausea_detail: Nausea, abdominal pain — may occur with some medications
medication_side_effects.footer: Raw figures entered by the patient. To be interpreted in consultation.
medication_side_effects.empty_text: Save your first entry to view the tracking.
medication_side_effects.events_empty: No events noted by your clinician.
medication_side_effects.instructions: Rate the intensity of each effect right now.
medication_side_effects.reminder_none: No reminder set by your clinician.
medication_side_effects.month_legend: The higher the average intensity for the day, the darker the circle (0 = light, 10 = dark).
medication_side_effects.markers_empty: No markers in this period. Mark a treatment event (new medication, dose change…) to place it on your charts.
medication_side_effects.markers_placeholder: E.g. switched to 150 mg, new medication…
medication_side_effects.already_saved: Today's entry already saved — you can edit it.
medication_side_effects.history_empty: Tracking will appear after your first entries.
medication_side_effects.custom_placeholder: Name of the effect to track…
medication_side_effects.config_hint: Choose the effects that concern you. You can also add a custom one.
medication_side_effects.config_empty_text: Tap “Configure tracked effects” to choose what to track.

medication_adherence.label: Treatment adherence
medication_adherence.description: Did I take my medication this week?
medication_adherence.title: Treatment adherence
medication_adherence.intro: Did you take your medication this week?
medication_adherence.status_missing_msg: Please indicate whether you took your medication.

psychoeducation.label: Psychoeducation
psychoeducation.description: Knowledge cards about your mental health
psychoeducation.title: Psychoeducation

sleep_diary.label: Sleep diary
sleep_diary.title: Sleep diary
sleep_diary.quality_missing_msg: Please rate the quality of your night (the stars).
sleep_diary.intro: Log your sleep every morning to track patterns over time.
sleep_diary.footer: CBT-I sleep diary — first-line treatment for chronic insomnia. Trauer et al., Ann Intern Med 2015 · NICE NG215 (2022).

mood_tracker.label: Mood thermometer
mood_tracker.description: Daily mood, energy and anxiety tracking (1–10)
mood_tracker.title: Mood thermometer
mood_tracker.intro: Log your mood, energy and anxiety for today.
mood_tracker.already_saved: Today's entry already saved — you can edit it.
mood_tracker.history_empty: History will appear once you have at least 2 entries.
mood_tracker.instructions: Rate each dimension for the present moment.
mood_tracker.empty_text: Record your first entry to see your history.
mood_tracker.footer: Raw figures reported by the patient. To be interpreted in consultation.
mood_tracker.reminder_none: No reminder configured by your therapist.
mood_tracker.markers_empty: No markers in this period. Mark an event (medication change, life event…) to place it on your charts.
mood_tracker.markers_placeholder: E.g.: started lithium, time off work…
mood_tracker.month_legend: The higher the day's average score, the darker the circle (1 = light, 10 = dark).

emotion_wheel.label: Emotion wheel
emotion_wheel.title: Emotion wheel
emotion_wheel.intro: Name your emotions precisely so you can recognise them and share them with your practitioner.
emotion_wheel.empty_text: Tap the button above to identify your first emotion.
emotion_wheel.step_primary_hint: Choose the emotion that comes closest.
emotion_wheel.step_secondary_hint: Pick the shade that fits best.
emotion_wheel.step_specific_hint: Refine your feeling with a more precise term.
emotion_wheel.step_intensity_hint: From 1 (very low) to 10 (very high). Raw value.
emotion_wheel.step_notes_hint: Context, situation, related thought… Free text.
emotion_wheel.footer: Emotion wheel — affect labelling (Plutchik's model). Lieberman et al., Psychol Sci 2007 · Kircanski, Lieberman & Craske, Psychol Sci 2012.

behavioral_activation.label: Behavioural activation
behavioral_activation.title: Behavioural activation
behavioral_activation.intro: Plan activities that bring pleasure or satisfaction.
behavioral_activation.empty_text:
Add activities to plan or that you've completed.
Rate each activity for Pleasure (P) and Mastery (M).
behavioral_activation.empty_description:
Add activities to plan or that you've completed.
Rate each activity for Pleasure (P) and Mastery (M).
behavioral_activation.month_hint_tap: Tap a day in the calendar to see its activities
behavioral_activation.activity_placeholder: E.g.: 20-min walk, Call a friend, Reading…

beck_columns.label: Beck columns
beck_columns.title: Beck columns
beck_columns.intro: Tap the button below to log your first automatic thought.
beck_columns.entry_col_1_hint: Where were you? What was happening? Who was there?
beck_columns.entry_col_3_hint: What thought came to mind at that moment?
beck_columns.entry_col_3_placeholder: E.g. I'm useless, this is going to go badly...
beck_columns.entry_col_3_belief: How much did you believe it?
beck_columns.entry_col_4_hint: Another way to see the situation? What facts contradict this thought?
beck_columns.entry_col_4_placeholder: Write a more balanced alternative thought...
beck_columns.entry_col_5_hint: How do you feel now, after this work?
beck_columns.entry_col_5_belief: How much do you believe this new thought?
beck_columns.empty_alert_msg: Please fill in at least the situation or the automatic thought.
beck_columns.footer: Cognitive restructuring journal (Beck's cognitive therapy). Hofmann et al., Cognit Ther Res 2012 · NICE NG222 (2022).

grounding.label: Grounding techniques
grounding.description: Return to the present with the 5-4-3-2-1 method
grounding.title: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
grounding.intro: This technique helps you reconnect with the present moment.

rim.label: RIM – Mental imagery
rim.title: RIM – Mental imagery
rim.empty_text:
Your therapist hasn't filled in your alternative scenario yet.
It will be available here after your next appointment.
rim.disclaimer: For use exclusively under the guidance of a healthcare professional trained in psychiatry or psychology.
rim.sounds_hint: Play a sound to accompany your reading. (Coming soon)
rim.protocol_step_1: Find a comfortable, quiet place, ideally lying down.
rim.protocol_step_2: Take a few slow, deep breaths before starting.
rim.protocol_step_3: Read your alternative scenario once or twice, taking your time.
rim.protocol_step_4: Close your eyes and mentally picture the images from the scenario.
rim.protocol_step_5: Practise every evening, preferably just before bed.
rim.footer: Mental Imagery Reprocessing (RIM) — Krakow et al., JAMA 2001 · Schmid et al., Psychother Psychosom 2021. Alternative scenario configured by the clinician, read-only for the patient.

fear_thermometer.label: Graded exposure
fear_thermometer.title: Graded exposure
fear_thermometer.intro: Rate your distress level on a scale from 0 to 100.
fear_thermometer.empty_text: Tap the button below to record your first distress measurement.
fear_thermometer.situations_hint: Name situations that trigger your fear to reuse them each time.
fear_thermometer.suds_hint_after: Fill in once the strategy is complete. You can come back later.
fear_thermometer.situation_missing_msg: Describe the trigger situation before saving.
fear_thermometer.saved_new_msg: Your distress measurement has been recorded.
fear_thermometer.save_error_msg: Could not save the entry. Please try again.
fear_thermometer.situation_catalogue_empty: No situations in your catalogue. Create some from the main screen.
fear_thermometer.strategies_hint: Select all the ones you applied.
fear_thermometer.footer: SUDS (Subjective Units of Distress Scale) — subjective distress measure used in CBT exposure therapy. Wolitzky-Taylor et al., Clin Psychol Rev 2008 · Craske et al., Behav Res Ther 2014.
fear_thermometer.disclaimer: This module supports your appointments. The techniques shown were discussed with your care provider — it does not replace your therapy.
fear_thermometer.ladder_empty_text: Add the situations that make you anxious, from the least to the most difficult.
fear_thermometer.step_label_placeholder: E.g. taking the subway at rush hour
fear_thermometer.detail_no_sessions: No exposure yet. Start one when you feel ready.
fear_thermometer.expectation_placeholder: What am I afraid will happen?
fear_thermometer.step_target_hint: How stressful is this situation before you start working on it? Used to order your ladder, from easiest to hardest.

breathing_techniques.label: Breathing techniques
breathing_techniques.title: Breathing techniques
breathing_techniques.coherence_cardiaque_description: Regulates the autonomic nervous system by synchronising breathing with heart rate variability (HRV). Recommended for anxiety, chronic stress and depression.
breathing_techniques.diaphragmatique_description: Activates the parasympathetic system. Foundation of most behavioural therapies.
breathing_techniques.carree_description: Symmetric technique used in acute stress and PTSD symptom management.
breathing_techniques.quatre_sept_huit_description: Short inhale, long hold, extended exhale. Effective for falling asleep and stopping acute anxiety.
breathing_techniques.pleine_conscience_description: Observe breathing without controlling it. Present-moment anchoring, reducing rumination. Core component of MBSR and MBCT, recommended by NICE for depression relapse prevention.
breathing_techniques.pleine_conscience_evidence: Grade A — NICE CG90, MBCT Segal et al. (2002)

cognitive_saturation.label: Cognitive saturation
cognitive_saturation.title: Cognitive saturation
cognitive_saturation.intro_text: Repeat a word or thought quickly and many times until it gradually loses its emotional charge and becomes 'just sounds'.
cognitive_saturation.empty_text: Tap the button above to start your first exercise.
cognitive_saturation.input_title: Which word or thought would you like to work on?
cognitive_saturation.input_hint: Choose a short word or brief thought that overwhelms you. Examples: 'worthless', 'danger', 'I'm going to fail'.
cognitive_saturation.input_placeholder: Enter a word or short thought…
cognitive_saturation.instructions:
1. Repeat your word out loud, as fast as possible.
2. Tap the word on each repetition to count.
3. Keep going for {{seconds}} seconds.

With enough repetitions, the word loses its meaning and emotional charge — this is the semantic saturation effect.
cognitive_saturation.done_text: Notice whether the word feels different to you now.

decisional_balance.label: Decisional balance
decisional_balance.title: Decisional balance
decisional_balance.behavior_placeholder: E.g. Take my medication every day

motivational_balance.label: Motivational balance
motivational_balance.disclaimer: This module supports your consultations. The techniques were introduced with your clinician — it does not replace your therapeutic follow-up.
motivational_balance.stage_subtitle: Select the stage that best describes you today.
motivational_balance.stage_1_desc: I don't see a need to change
motivational_balance.stage_2_desc: I'm thinking about it, but haven't decided
motivational_balance.stage_6_desc: I've returned to the old habit — this is normal
motivational_balance.rulers_importance_q: How important is it for you to change?
motivational_balance.rulers_importance_why_label: Why that score and not lower?
motivational_balance.rulers_confidence_q: If you decided to change, how confident would you feel?
motivational_balance.rulers_confidence_why_label: What would make you more confident?
motivational_balance.rulers_confidence_why_placeholder: What I would need to believe in it…
motivational_balance.rulers_commitment_placeholder: In one sentence, what I take away from this session…
motivational_balance.balance_values_subtitle: Choose up to 3 values — they will anchor your balance.
motivational_balance.balance_commitment_placeholder: In one sentence, what I take away from today…
motivational_balance.balance_no_items: No arguments yet. Add reasons to change or barriers.

phq9.label: PHQ-9
phq9.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first PHQ-9.
phq9.instructions_2: how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems?
phq9.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phq9.q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
phq9.q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
phq9.q6: Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
phq9.q7: Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
phq9.q8: Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed. Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
phq9.q9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
phq9.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

bsl23.label: BSL-23
bsl23.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first BSL-23.
bsl23.instructions_2: how much have you suffered from the following problems?
bsl23.q3: Feeling uncomfortable in my own skin
bsl23.q5: Feeling like I'm outside my own body
bsl23.q8: Being unable to express my feelings
bsl23.q9: Thinking about hurting or injuring myself
bsl23.q10: Feeling as though I don't exist
bsl23.q12: Feeling so bad that I want to hurt myself
bsl23.q13: Feeling so tense that I feel like I'm going to explode
bsl23.q14: Feeling agitated and unable to calm down
bsl23.q18: Finding it hard to believe that others respect me
bsl23.q20: Feeling angry and irritable for no apparent reason
bsl23.q21: Feeling that others see me negatively
bsl23.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

gad7.label: GAD-7
gad7.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first GAD-7.
gad7.instructions_2: how often have you been bothered by the following problems?
gad7.q1: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
gad7.q2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
gad7.q3: Worrying too much about different things
gad7.q5: Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
gad7.q7: Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen
gad7.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

rcads.label: RCADS-25
rcads.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first RCADS-25.
rcads.instructions: For each of the following statements, please indicate how often this happens for you.
rcads.footer: This questionnaire is a monitoring tool. Only your practitioner can interpret the results.
rcads.q1: I worry when I think I have done poorly at something.
rcads.q2: I feel scared that something bad will happen to me.
rcads.q3: I worry about being as good as other kids.
rcads.q5: I am scared of going to school.
rcads.q6: Suddenly I feel as if I can't breathe when there is no reason for this.
rcads.q9: Suddenly I feel really scared for no reason at all.
rcads.q10: I don't want to be away from my family.
rcads.q11: I feel scared to sleep away from home.
rcads.q12: I worry when someone is late.
rcads.q13: I don't want to go to school because I miss my parents.
rcads.q14: I feel scared that something bad will happen to someone in my family.
rcads.q15: I feel nervous when I have to perform in front of people.
rcads.q16: I feel scared that I will do something embarrassing in front of people.
rcads.q17: I worry about what other people think of me.
rcads.q18: I feel scared when I have to talk in front of the class.
rcads.q19: I have unpleasant thoughts that keep coming back.
rcads.q20: I have to keep doing things over and over (like washing my hands, cleaning, or putting things in order) so that bad things won't happen.
rcads.q21: I don't enjoy things as much as before.
rcads.q23: I feel tired or lack energy.
rcads.q25: I have lost interest in things I used to enjoy.

epds.label: EPDS
epds.instructions: You have recently had a baby. We would like to know how you are feeling. Please underline the answer which comes closest to how you have felt in the past 7 days.
epds.q1: I have been able to laugh and see the funny side of things
epds.q1_opt0: As much as I always could
epds.q2: I have looked forward with enjoyment to things
epds.q2_opt0: As much as I ever did
epds.q2_opt1: Rather less than I used to
epds.q2_opt2: Definitely less than I used to
epds.q3: I have blamed myself unnecessarily when things went wrong
epds.q4: I have been anxious or worried for no good reason
epds.q5: I have felt scared or panicky for no very good reason
epds.q6: Things have been getting on top of me
epds.q6_opt0: Yes, most of the time I haven't been able to cope at all
epds.q6_opt1: Yes, sometimes I haven't been coping as well as usual
epds.q6_opt2: No, most of the time I have coped quite well
epds.q6_opt3: No, I have been coping as well as ever
epds.q7: I have been so unhappy that I have had difficulty sleeping
epds.q8: I have felt sad or miserable
epds.q9: I have been so unhappy that I have been crying
epds.q10: The thought of harming myself has occurred to me
epds.footer: The score is shared with your healthcare provider. Its interpretation is their responsibility.
epds.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first EPDS.

nsi.label: NSI
nsi.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first NSI.
nsi.instructions: For each question, indicate the number that best matches your experience over the past month (0 = least / 5 = most):
nsi.q1: How often your bad dreams wake you up
nsi.q2: The emotional intensity of your nightmares
nsi.q3: How often your nightmares involve life threats to your physical safety or integrity
nsi.q4: How much your nightmares disrupt your overall daytime functioning
nsi.q5: How much your nightmares disrupt your daytime mood
nsi.q6: How much your nightmares disrupt your daytime concentration and memory
nsi.q7: How much your nightmares affect your ability to stay awake (daytime sleepiness)
nsi.q8: How much your nightmares disrupt your ability to fall asleep
nsi.q9: How much your nightmares disrupt the continuity of your sleep
nsi.pct_question: 10. What percentage of your nightmares are recurring nightmares?
nsi.themes_question: 11. What are the recurring theme(s) of your nightmares?
nsi.footer: The score is shared with your care provider. Interpretation is their responsibility.

snap_iv.label: SNAP-IV
snap_iv.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first SNAP-IV.
snap_iv.instructions_1: Hetero-evaluation — to be filled in by a parent or teacher.
snap_iv.instructions_2: For each of the following behaviours, indicate how often you observe it in the child.
snap_iv.warning: This questionnaire must be completed by a parent, guardian or teacher — not by the child.
snap_iv.q1: Fails to give close attention to details or makes careless mistakes in schoolwork, work, or other activities.
snap_iv.q2: Has difficulty sustaining attention in tasks or play activities.
snap_iv.q3: Does not seem to listen when spoken to directly.
snap_iv.q4: Does not follow through on instructions and fails to finish schoolwork, chores, or duties (not due to oppositional behaviour or failure to understand).
snap_iv.q5: Has difficulty organising tasks and activities.
snap_iv.q6: Avoids, dislikes, or is reluctant to engage in tasks that require sustained mental effort.
snap_iv.q7: Loses things necessary for tasks or activities (pencils, books, tools, assignments).
snap_iv.q8: Is easily distracted by extraneous stimuli.
snap_iv.q10: Fidgets with hands or feet or squirms in seat.
snap_iv.q11: Leaves seat in classroom or in other situations in which remaining seated is expected.
snap_iv.q12: Runs about or climbs excessively in situations in which it is inappropriate.
snap_iv.q13: Has difficulty playing or engaging in leisure activities quietly.
snap_iv.q14: Is 'on the go' or often acts as if 'driven by a motor'.
snap_iv.q16: Blurts out answers before questions have been completed.
snap_iv.q18: Interrupts or intrudes on others (butts into conversations or games).
snap_iv.q21: Actively defies or refuses to comply with adults' requests or rules.
snap_iv.q23: Blames others for his or her mistakes or misbehaviour.
snap_iv.q24: Is touchy or easily annoyed by others.
snap_iv.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

asrs6.label: ASRS v1.1 — Screener
asrs6.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first ASRS-6.
asrs6.instructions_2: for each of the following questions, select the option that best describes how you have felt and conducted yourself.
asrs6.q1: How often do you have difficulty wrapping up the final details of a project, once the challenging parts have been done?
asrs6.q2: How often do you have difficulty getting things in order when you have to do a task that requires organisation?
asrs6.q3: How often do you have problems remembering appointments or obligations?
asrs6.q4: When you have a task that requires a lot of thought, how often do you avoid or delay getting started?
asrs6.q5: How often do you fidget or squirm with your hands or feet when you have to sit down for a long time?
asrs6.q6: How often do you feel overly active and compelled to do things, like you were driven by a motor?
asrs6.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

asrs18.label: ASRS v1.1 — Full Assessment
asrs18.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first ASRS-18.
asrs18.instructions_2: for each of the following questions, select the option that best describes how you have felt and conducted yourself.
asrs18.q1: How often do you have difficulty wrapping up the final details of a project, once the challenging parts have been done?
asrs18.q2: How often do you have difficulty getting things in order when you have to do a task that requires organisation?
asrs18.q3: How often do you have problems remembering appointments or obligations?
asrs18.q4: When you have a task that requires a lot of thought, how often do you avoid or delay getting started?
asrs18.q5: How often do you fidget or squirm with your hands or feet when you have to sit down for a long time?
asrs18.q6: How often do you feel overly active and compelled to do things, like you were driven by a motor?
asrs18.q7: How often do you make careless mistakes when you have to work on a boring or difficult project?
asrs18.q8: How often do you have difficulty keeping your attention when you are doing boring or repetitive work?
asrs18.q9: How often do you have difficulty concentrating on what people say to you, even when they are speaking to you directly?
asrs18.q10: How often do you misplace or have difficulty finding things at home or at work?
asrs18.q11: How often are you distracted by activity or noise around you?
asrs18.q12: How often do you leave your seat in meetings or other situations in which you are expected to remain seated?
asrs18.q13: How often do you feel restless or fidgety?
asrs18.q14: How often do you have difficulty unwinding and relaxing when you have time to yourself?
asrs18.q15: How often do you find yourself talking too much when you are in social situations?
asrs18.q16: When you're in a conversation, how often do you find yourself finishing the sentences of the people you are talking to, before they can finish them themselves?
asrs18.q17: How often do you have difficulty waiting your turn in situations when turn-taking is required?
asrs18.q18: How often do you interrupt others when they are busy?
asrs18.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

agenda.empty_description: Your upcoming appointments will appear here.
agenda.empty_week: No available slots on this day.

appointment.cancel_confirm: Are you sure you want to cancel this appointment?

notifications.all_empty: Your care provider hasn't set up any reminders yet.

onboarding.title: Stay supported between your sessions
onboarding.intro: With your consent, Kær can send reminders to your phone.
onboarding.benefit_exercises_title: Your exercises at the right time
onboarding.benefit_exercises_text: A discreet reminder at the times set with your care provider, so you don't forget your therapeutic exercises.
onboarding.benefit_appointments_text: A notification before your upcoming sessions with your practitioner.
onboarding.benefit_control_text: You can adjust reminder times or pause them at any moment, from each module.
onboarding.privacy_note: The content of your exercises is never included in a notification. Reminders are scheduled in advance, never triggered by what you enter.
onboarding.skip_hint: You can enable them later in your phone settings.
